_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Infected Insect Bites. Based on NICE CKS and Pharmacy First criteria.
label: Infected Insect Bites

questions.text: Is the bite area red, swollen, warm, or oozing?
Is the redness spreading beyond the immediate bite area?
Do you have a fever (temperature above 38°C) or feel generally unwell?
Is there a red line tracking up from the bite toward your body?
Do you have difficulty breathing, swelling of the face or throat, or a severe rash all over your body?
Were you bitten while abroad (possible exotic tick or insect bite)?
Do you have any known allergies to antibiotics?

redFlags.description: Anaphylaxis — breathing difficulty, facial/throat swelling, widespread rash
redFlags.message: These symptoms could be anaphylaxis — a life-threatening allergic reaction. Call 999 immediately.

redFlags.description: Red line tracking from bite — possible lymphangitis (blood poisoning)
redFlags.message: A red line spreading from a bite is a medical emergency. Go to A&E or call 999 immediately.

redFlags.description: Spreading cellulitis with systemic infection signs
redFlags.message: Spreading infection with fever needs same-day medical assessment.

redFlags.description: Bite from abroad — possible tick-borne illness or tropical disease
redFlags.message: Bites from abroad may carry tropical diseases. Please see your GP today.

eligibilityRules.condition: q2 === true && q3 === false && q4 === false
eligibilityRules.reason: Localised bite infection without spreading or systemic signs — pharmacy eligible

outcomeRules.reason: No signs of infection — self-care appropriate
Localised infected insect bite — pharmacy treatment appropriate

selfCareAdvice: Clean the area with soap and water. Apply a cold compress. Avoid scratching. Antihistamine cream or oral antihistamine for itching. Monitor for spreading redness.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Flucloxacillin 500mg four times daily for 5–7 days
Clarithromycin 500mg twice daily for 5 days (penicillin allergy)
safetyNetAdvice: Return urgently if: redness spreads significantly, a red line appears, fever develops, or you feel very unwell.
